14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
20	#
21	# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
22	# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
23	# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
24	# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Command Functions
32	
33	# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
34	# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
35	bujb_resolve() {
36	  zbujb_sentinel
37	  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
38	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
39	
40	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
41	
42	  bujb_resolve_investiture
43	
44	  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
45	  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
46	  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
47	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
48	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
49	  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
50	  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
51	}
52	
53	# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
54	bujb_knock() {
55	  zbujb_sentinel
56	  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
57	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
58	
59	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
60	
61	  bujb_resolve_investiture
62	
63	  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
64	
65	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
66	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
67	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
68	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
69	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
70	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
71	      true                                        \
72	    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"
73	
74	  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
75	}
76	
77	# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
78	# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
79	# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
80	# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
81	bujb_command_file() {
82	  zbujb_sentinel
83	  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
84	  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
85	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
86	
87	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
88	
89	  local -r z_command_file="${1:-}"
90	  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
91	  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
92	  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"
93	
94	  bujb_resolve_investiture
95	
96	  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"
97	  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
98	
99	  local z_exit=0
100	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
101	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
102	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
103	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
104	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
105	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
106	      'bash -s'                                   \
107	      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
108	      > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDOUT}"                  \
109	      2> "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"                 \
110	    || z_exit=$?
111	
112	  echo "${z_exit}" > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}" \
113	    || buc_die "Failed to write exit-code capture: ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}"
114	
115	  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
116	    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"
117	
118	  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
119	  buc_step "Capture complete"
120	}
121	
122	# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
123	# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
124	bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
125	  zbujb_sentinel
126	  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
127	  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
128	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
129	
130	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
131	  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die
132	
133	  bujb_resolve_investiture
134	  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
135	}
136	
137	# bujb_caparison_windows_command - caparison ceremony, Windows admin host posture.
138	bujb_caparison_windows_command() {
139	  zbujb_sentinel
140	  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_windows node (admin SSH trust, sshd harden, WSL stage, sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start)"
141	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
142	
143	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
144	  bujb_resolve_investiture
145	  bujb_caparison_windows
146	}
147	
148	# bujb_caparison_macos_command - caparison ceremony, macOS admin host posture.
149	bujb_caparison_macos_command() {
150	  zbujb_sentinel
151	  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_mac node (Remote Login, pmset, tailscaled)"
152	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
153	
154	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
155	  bujb_resolve_investiture
156	  bujb_caparison_macos
157	}
158	
159	# bujb_caparison_linux_command - caparison ceremony, Linux admin host posture.
160	bujb_caparison_linux_command() {
161	  zbujb_sentinel
162	  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_linux node (sshd, sleep mask, tailscaled)"
163	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
164	
165	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
166	  bujb_resolve_investiture
167	  bujb_caparison_linux
168	}
169	
170	# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
171	bujb_garrison_bash() {
172	  zbujb_sentinel
173	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
174	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
175	
176	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
177	  bujb_resolve_investiture
178	  bujb_garrison b
179	}
180	
181	# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
182	bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
183	  zbujb_sentinel
184	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
185	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
186	
187	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
188	  bujb_resolve_investiture
189	  bujb_garrison c
190	}
191	
192	# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
193	bujb_garrison_wsl() {
194	  zbujb_sentinel
195	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
196	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
197	
198	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
199	  bujb_resolve_investiture
200	  bujb_garrison w
201	}
202	
203	# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
204	bujb_interactive_session() {
205	  zbujb_sentinel
206	  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
207	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
208	
209	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
210	
211	  bujb_resolve_investiture
212	
213	  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
214	
215	  exec ssh -t                                     \
216	      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
217	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
218	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
219	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \

222	
223	######################################################################
224	# Furnish and Main

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDOUT}"                  \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"                 \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write exit-code capture: ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die

  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
}

# bujb_caparison_windows_command - caparison ceremony, Windows admin host posture.
bujb_caparison_windows_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_windows node (admin SSH trust, sshd harden, WSL stage, sleep disable, Tailscale auto-start)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_caparison_windows
}

# bujb_caparison_macos_command - caparison ceremony, macOS admin host posture.
bujb_caparison_macos_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_mac node (Remote Login, pmset, tailscaled)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_caparison_macos
}

# bujb_caparison_linux_command - caparison ceremony, Linux admin host posture.
bujb_caparison_linux_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Caparison — admin host posture for a bubep_linux node (sshd, sleep mask, tailscaled)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_caparison_linux
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

1475	bujb_garrison() {
1476	  zbujb_sentinel
1477	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
1478	    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
1479	
1480	  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
1481	  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"
1482	
1483	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"
1484	
1485	  bujp_preflight                     "${z_letter}"
1486	
1487	  # Reboot precondition (Windows only): guarantees a clean HKEY_USERS
1488	  # baseline so obliterate Phase 2 Win32_UserProfile delete is not
1489	  # blocked by a sticky-loaded workload hive from a prior SSH-as-
1490	  # workload session. Reboot is the canonical Windows state-reset
1491	  # primitive; see zbujb_reboot_and_await_ssh for the rationale.
1492	  case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
1493	    bubep_windows) zbujb_reboot_and_await_ssh ;;
1494	  esac
1495	
1496	  zbujb_obliterate_workload
1497	  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
1498	
1499	  case "${z_letter}" in
1500	    w)
1501	      # Order is load-bearing:
1502	      #   1. place_bare_trust BEFORE the workload sessions (each session
1503	      #      authenticates against this file via caparison's Match block).
1504	      #   2. export_seed BEFORE session_import (import consumes the seed).
1505	      #   3. Four workload sessions in order (import → useradd → privkey →
1506	      #      shutdown). Each one wsl.exe call; final shutdown releases
1507	      #      hive handles before workload's Windows logon terminates.
1508	      #   4. lockdown_rewrite AFTER the four sessions (subsequent workload
1509	      #      SSH gets the command= form). Admin context, single Set/Write.
1510	      #   5. seed_cleanup last (admin removes the tarball).
1511	      zbujb_garrison_w_place_bare_trust
1512	      zbujb_garrison_w_export_seed
1513	      zbujb_garrison_w_session_import
1514	      zbujb_garrison_w_session_useradd
1515	      zbujb_garrison_w_session_privkey
1516	      zbujb_garrison_w_session_shutdown
1517	      zbujb_garrison_w_lockdown_rewrite
1518	      zbujb_garrison_w_seed_cleanup
1519	      ;;
1520	    b|c)
1521	      zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust "${z_letter}"
1522	      zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key   "${z_letter}"
1523	      ;;
1524	  esac
1525	
1526	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
1527	}
1528	
1529	######################################################################
1530	# Internal: Caparison-windows helpers (BUSJCW — Windows OpenSSH only)
1531	#
1532	# Phase 1 decomposes into 13 single-cmdlet steps per WSG WSp-105 bounded
1533	# by a key-auth CDD probe: zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' decides
1534	# whether the bounded password-fallback Add-Content (step 3) runs via
1535	# zbujb_admin_powershell_with_password_fallback or no-ops. Every other
1536	# step is key-only via zbujb_admin_powershell. Phase 2 reconnects under
1537	# key-only auth and re-verifies the served sshd_config. No
1538	# ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.
1539	
1540	# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
1541	# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
1542	# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
1543	# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
1544	# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
1545	zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives() {
1546	  zbujb_sentinel
1547	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
1548	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file required"
1549	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
1550	
1551	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
1552	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
1553	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
1554	
1555	  local z_directives_roll=()
1556	  local z_pair=""
1557	  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
1558	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
1559	    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
1560	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
1561	
1562	  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
1563	  local z_line=""
1564	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
1565	    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
1566	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
1567	
1568	  local z_directive=""
1569	  local z_expected=""
1570	  local z_actual=""
1571	  local z_after_directive=""
1572	  local z_i=0
1573	  local z_j=0
1574	  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
1575	    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
1576	    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
1577	    z_actual=""
1578

file.content:
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  bujp_preflight                     "${z_letter}"

  # Reboot precondition (Windows only): guarantees a clean HKEY_USERS
  # baseline so obliterate Phase 2 Win32_UserProfile delete is not
  # blocked by a sticky-loaded workload hive from a prior SSH-as-
  # workload session. Reboot is the canonical Windows state-reset
  # primitive; see zbujb_reboot_and_await_ssh for the rationale.
  case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
    bubep_windows) zbujb_reboot_and_await_ssh ;;
  esac

  zbujb_obliterate_workload
  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    w)
      # Order is load-bearing:
      #   1. place_bare_trust BEFORE the workload sessions (each session
      #      authenticates against this file via caparison's Match block).
      #   2. export_seed BEFORE session_import (import consumes the seed).
      #   3. Four workload sessions in order (import → useradd → privkey →
      #      shutdown). Each one wsl.exe call; final shutdown releases
      #      hive handles before workload's Windows logon terminates.
      #   4. lockdown_rewrite AFTER the four sessions (subsequent workload
      #      SSH gets the command= form). Admin context, single Set/Write.
      #   5. seed_cleanup last (admin removes the tarball).
      zbujb_garrison_w_place_bare_trust
      zbujb_garrison_w_export_seed
      zbujb_garrison_w_session_import
      zbujb_garrison_w_session_useradd
      zbujb_garrison_w_session_privkey
      zbujb_garrison_w_session_shutdown
      zbujb_garrison_w_lockdown_rewrite
      zbujb_garrison_w_seed_cleanup
      ;;
    b|c)
      zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust "${z_letter}"
      zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key   "${z_letter}"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Caparison-windows helpers (BUSJCW — Windows OpenSSH only)
#
# Phase 1 decomposes into 13 single-cmdlet steps per WSG WSp-105 bounded
# by a key-auth CDD probe: zbujb_admin_powershell 'exit 0' decides
# whether the bounded password-fallback Add-Content (step 3) runs via
# zbujb_admin_powershell_with_password_fallback or no-ops. Every other
# step is key-only via zbujb_admin_powershell. Phase 2 reconnects under
# key-only auth and re-verifies the served sshd_config. No
# ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.

# zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file required"
  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"

  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_caparison_windows_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"

  local z_directives_roll=()
  local z_pair=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"

  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"

  local z_directive=""
  local z_expected=""
  local z_actual=""
  local z_after_directive=""
  local z_i=0
  local z_j=0
  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
    z_actual=""

    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
        "${z_directive} "*)
          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
          break
          ;;
      esac
    done

    test -n "${z_actual}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
  done
}

# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
# Tinder-on-tinder source-time interpolation: cygwin/wsl/distribution/user
# resolve at source time so the value is a fully-baked literal by the time
# bujb_command_for_capture echoes it. \"...\" pairs are literal escaped
# quotes the remote sshd parser expects around the command directive's
# argument; \$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND defers expansion to the remote shell.
BUJB_command_b="command=\"${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
BUJB_command_c="command=\"${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login -c \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
BUJB_command_w="command=\"${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec ${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"

# Workload privkey destination path on the remote (relative to the workload
# account home directory). Uniform across shell-letters under the current
# design — if a future shell needs a divergent keypath, fan out per-letter.
BUJB_workload_keypath='.ssh/id_ed25519'

# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
# caparison-windows phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
PasswordAuthentication no
PermitEmptyPasswords no'

# User-provisioning argument sets per platform tooling.
#
# BUJB_useradd_workload_args — Linux/WSL useradd flags declaring the
# workload account policy (home-directory creation + absolute shell).
# Used in step3 b (linux), step3 b (mac via dscl UserShell), and
# w_init wsl-useradd.
# BUJB_netuser_add_args — Windows net.exe user /add flags declaring the
# password-not-required + active workload posture (used in step3 c and

1233	# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
1234	# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
1235	zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
1236	  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
1237	  local z_wlhome
1238	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
1239	    || buc_die "step5: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
1240	  local -r z_target="${z_wlhome}/${BUJB_workload_keypath}"
1241	  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"
1242	
1243	  # Single ssh-stdin pipeline per shell (pace ₢A-AA9): the workload key
1244	  # file flows from BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE on the curia, over ssh stdin
1245	  # (FD 0), into the remote shell, into install's /dev/stdin, and lands
1246	  # at z_target with mode + owner atomically set by install -D. No
1247	  # plaintext on either side's disk; no remote temp file, trap, or
1248	  # mktemp; no curia-side base64 encode; no key material in argv.
1249	  # See WSG SH-10 for the single-statement-body contract.
1250	  case "${z_letter}" in
1251	    b)
1252	      # Linux/Mac: privileged user needs sudo to write into workload's
1253	      # home; both -o and -g supplied because useradd auto-mints a
1254	      # self-named primary group on Linux/Mac.
1255	      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}5-b-install-" \
1256	          "sudo -n install -D -m 600 -o '${BUJB_workload_user}' -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_path_devstdin} '${z_target}'" \
1257	          < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
1258	        || buc_die "step5 (b): failed to plant workload key at ${z_target}"
1259	      ;;
1260	    w)
1261	      # WSL: wsl.exe --user root provides root inside the distribution,
1262	      # no sudo needed. -o and -g supplied (WSL distro is Linux-flavoured;
1263	      # useradd inside it mints a self-named group).
1264	      zbujb_admin_exec_wsl "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}5-w-install-" \
1265	          "install -D -m 600 -o '${BUJB_workload_user}' -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_path_devstdin} '${z_target}'" \
1266	          < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
1267	        || buc_die "step5 (w): failed to plant workload key at ${z_target}"
1268	      ;;
1269	    c)
1270	      # Cygwin: privileged user is a Windows admin; chown works via NTFS
1271	      # ACL without sudo. -g omitted because Windows accounts (minted via
1272	      # net.exe user /add) do not get an auto-self-named group; passing
1273	      # -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' would fail.

zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${BUJB_workload_user})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux)
          zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-linux-useradd-" \
              "sudo -n useradd ${BUJB_useradd_workload_args} '${BUJB_workload_user}'" \
            || buc_die "step3 (b/linux): useradd failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
          zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-linux-passwd-lock-" \
              "sudo -n passwd --lock '${BUJB_workload_user}'" \
            || buc_die "step3 (b/linux): passwd --lock failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
          ;;
        bubep_mac)
          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
          zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-mac-adduser-" \
              "sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${BUJB_workload_user}' -roleAccount" \
            || buc_die "step3 (b/mac): sysadminctl -addUser failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
          zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-mac-dscl-shell-" \
              "sudo -n dscl . -create '${BUJB_path_mac_user_home}' UserShell ${BUJB_shell_bash}" \
            || buc_die "step3 (b/mac): dscl UserShell create failed for ${BUJB_path_mac_user_home}"
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-c-netuser-add-" \
          "net.exe user '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_netuser_add_args} > /dev/null" \
        || buc_die "step3 (c): net.exe user /add failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-c-mkpasswd-" \
          "mkpasswd -l -u '${BUJB_workload_user}' >> /etc/passwd" \
        || buc_die "step3 (c): mkpasswd append to /etc/passwd failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-c-mkdir-home-" \
          "mkdir -p '${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}'" \
        || buc_die "step3 (c): mkdir of ${BUJB_path_posix_user_home} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-c-chown-home-" \
          "chown -R '${BUJB_workload_user}' '${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}'" \
        || buc_die "step3 (c): chown of ${BUJB_path_posix_user_home} failed"
      ;;
    w)
      # Windows-side workload account only. The WSL-side Linux user is
      # provisioned later inside the workload's own imported rbtww-main
      # distribution by zbujb_garrison_w_session_useradd.
      #
      # No ProfileList registry write: absolute-path AuthorizedKeysFile
      # (caparison-installed Match User block) decouples auth from
      # workload-profile state. The workload's first SSH session
      # (zbujb_garrison_w_session_import) creates the profile naturally
      # via User Profile Service.
      zbujb_admin_exec_wsl "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}3-w-netuser-add-" \
          "net.exe user '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_netuser_add_args} > /dev/null" \
        || buc_die "step3 (w): net.exe user /add failed for ${BUJB_workload_user}"
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|c) ;;
    *)   buc_die "step4_place_trust: letter must be b or c (got '${z_letter}'); w letter uses zbujb_garrison_w_place_bare_trust" ;;
  esac

  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
    || buc_die "step4: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"

  local -r z_authkeys_dir="${z_wlhome}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename})"

  local z_command_directive
  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for_capture "${z_letter}") \
    || buc_die "step4: bujb_command_for_capture failed for letter='${z_letter}'"

  ${BUJB_sshkeygen_emit_pubkey} "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
      > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}" \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}"
  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}")
  z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"

  local -r z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"$'\n'

  local z_sudo_prefix=""
  test "${z_letter}" != "b" || z_sudo_prefix="sudo -n "

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-b-mkdir-" \
          "${z_sudo_prefix}mkdir -p '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (b): mkdir of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-b-chmod-dir-" \
          "${z_sudo_prefix}chmod 700 '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (b): chmod 700 of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-b-write-authkeys-" \
          "echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | ${z_sudo_prefix}tee '${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}' > /dev/null" \
        || buc_die "step4 (b): write to ${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-b-chmod-file-" \
          "${z_sudo_prefix}chmod 600 '${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (b): chmod 600 of ${BUJB_authkeys_basename} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-b-chown-" \
          "${z_sudo_prefix}chown -R '${BUJB_workload_user}:${BUJB_workload_user}' '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (b): chown of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      ;;
    c)
      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-c-mkdir-" \
          "mkdir -p '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (c): mkdir of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-c-chmod-dir-" \
          "chmod 700 '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (c): chmod 700 of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-c-write-authkeys-" \
          "echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (c): write to ${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-c-chmod-file-" \
          "chmod 600 '${z_authkeys_dir}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (c): chmod 600 of ${BUJB_authkeys_basename} failed"
      zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}4-c-chown-" \
          "chown -R '${BUJB_workload_user}' '${z_authkeys_dir}'" \
        || buc_die "step4 (c): chown of ${z_authkeys_dir} failed"
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
    || buc_die "step5: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
  local -r z_target="${z_wlhome}/${BUJB_workload_keypath}"
  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"

  # Single ssh-stdin pipeline per shell (pace ₢A-AA9): the workload key
  # file flows from BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE on the curia, over ssh stdin
  # (FD 0), into the remote shell, into install's /dev/stdin, and lands
  # at z_target with mode + owner atomically set by install -D. No
  # plaintext on either side's disk; no remote temp file, trap, or
  # mktemp; no curia-side base64 encode; no key material in argv.
  # See WSG SH-10 for the single-statement-body contract.
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      # Linux/Mac: privileged user needs sudo to write into workload's
      # home; both -o and -g supplied because useradd auto-mints a
      # self-named primary group on Linux/Mac.
      zbujb_admin_exec_native "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}5-b-install-" \
          "sudo -n install -D -m 600 -o '${BUJB_workload_user}' -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_path_devstdin} '${z_target}'" \
          < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
        || buc_die "step5 (b): failed to plant workload key at ${z_target}"
      ;;
    w)
      # WSL: wsl.exe --user root provides root inside the distribution,
      # no sudo needed. -o and -g supplied (WSL distro is Linux-flavoured;
      # useradd inside it mints a self-named group).
      zbujb_admin_exec_wsl "${ZBUJB_CHIT_garrison_step}5-w-install-" \
          "install -D -m 600 -o '${BUJB_workload_user}' -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' ${BUJB_path_devstdin} '${z_target}'" \
          < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
        || buc_die "step5 (w): failed to plant workload key at ${z_target}"
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin: privileged user is a Windows admin; chown works via NTFS
      # ACL without sudo. -g omitted because Windows accounts (minted via
      # net.exe user /add) do not get an auto-self-named group; passing
      # -g '${BUJB_workload_user}' would fail.

#                     Use this form inside sshd_config text.
#   $env:ProgramData  PowerShell expansion form; use for PS cmdlet arguments.
BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys="__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/${BUJB_workload_user}/authorized_keys"
BUJB_path_ps_workload_authkeys_dir="\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
BUJB_path_ps_workload_authkeys="${BUJB_path_ps_workload_authkeys_dir}\\authorized_keys"

# sshd_config Match block content for the workload user. Caparison appends
# this to the end of sshd_config; idempotency strips any prior copy before
# the append. Two-line literal — sshd accepts the indented continuation.
BUJB_sshd_match_block_text="Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}
    AuthorizedKeysFile ${BUJB_path_sshd_workload_authkeys}"

# WSL-install seed distribution: the Microsoft-published distribution that
# zbujb_caparison_windows_stage_wsl fetches via `wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d`, exports
# to .tar, then re-imports under BUJB_wsl_distribution.
BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution='Ubuntu-24.04'

# Windows-side install directory housing both seed and canonical
# distribution VHD trees plus the intermediate .tar export. The
# bujb- prefix names the BUK module that owns this directory, so
# the path is unambiguously project-controlled rather than reading
# as a Microsoft-default location (the prior C:\WSL value did).
BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\bujb-wsl'

# WSL artifacts.
# Seed tarball lives under C:\bujb-wsl (caparison-windows-created;
# inherits BUILTIN\Users:ReadAndExecute from C:\ default ACL, which
# is what allows the SSH-as-workload `wsl --import` to read the
# seed without an icacls fix-up step). Writing the seed under the
# workload profile would inherit admin's logon-SID ACL excluding
# the workload user, since admin runs the wsl --export. The
# workload's installed-VHD root stays under the workload profile
# (workload owns it; created by the workload's own wsl --import).
BUJB_path_win_seed_tarball="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_seed_basename}"
BUJB_path_win_wsl_root="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_wsl_root_basename}"

# Windows ACL principal names used in icacls /grant arguments. The :F
# permission suffix is appended at use sites alongside ${BUJB_workload_user}:F
# so all three principals follow the same `<principal>:F` shape.
BUJB_acl_principal_system='SYSTEM'
BUJB_acl_principal_admins='BUILTIN\Administrators'

# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.

content.text:
I have a clear picture. Here's what I found and the proposed hack.

## How the existing SSH paths work

**Two SSH targets per node**, distinguished by which key:

| Path | Tabtarget | User | Key | Remote shell |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Admin | `tt/buw-jpS bujn-winpc <cmd>` | `bhyslop` (BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER) | `~/.ssh/id_ed25519_winpc-admin` | Whatever Windows OpenSSH `DefaultShell` returns (cmd/PowerShell) |
| Workload | `tt/buw-jws bujn-winpc` | `bujuw_user` (BUJB_workload_user, hardcoded) | `~/.ssh/id_ed25519_winpc-workload` | **Determined by `command=` directive in remote `authorized_keys`** |

BURN profile for rocket: `.buk/rbmn_nodes/bujn-winpc/burn.env` — just `BURN_HOST=rocket`, `BURN_PLATFORM=bubep_windows`.
BURP profile (your keys): `.buk/rbmu_users/bhyslop/bujn-winpc/burp.env` — three fields, no surprises.

**The single load-bearing trick** for "ssh lands in Cygwin": `BUJB_command_c` (bujb_jurisdiction.sh:167) is a forced-command directive

```
command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,...
```

prepended to the workload pubkey in `authorized_keys`. Any incoming SSH session for that user is wrapped by `cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c <whatever-the-client-asked-for>`. `tt/buw-jws` asks for `bash -i`, so you get an interactive Cygwin bash.

## What garrison-c actually wraps around that one trick

`bujb_garrison c` (bujb_jurisdiction.sh:1475) is a **6-step destructive ceremony** doing far more than placing that one line:

| Step | What it does | Needed for the hack? |
|---|---|---|
| `bujp_preflight c` | Asserts admin SSH key auth works, validates BURN/BURP fields | Yes (or do it by hand once) |
| `zbujb_reboot_and_await_ssh` | **Reboots rocket** to clear sticky HKEY_USERS state | No |
| `zbujb_obliterate_workload` | Destroys existing `bujuw_user` account + profile + Win32_UserProfile entries | No |
| Step 3 (`c`) | `net.exe user bujuw_user /add` + `mkpasswd >> /etc/passwd` + mkdir/chown home | **One-time, yes** |
| Step 4 (`c`) | Write `authorized_keys` with the `command="...cygwin64/bin/bash..."` directive | **One-time, yes** |
| Step 5 (`c`) | Plant workload privkey on remote (for outbound to GitHub) | Only if remote needs to push |

**Caparison Phase 1** (BUS-config hardening, sshd_config Match block routing the workload's authorized_keys to `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\bujuw_user\authorized_keys`) is **not strictly required** if you put the authorized_keys at the Windows OpenSSH default location for non-admins: `C:\Users\bujuw_user\.ssh\authorized_keys`.

## The hack

Three one-time manual operator steps on rocket (you have admin SSH already, run from your Mac via `tt/buw-jpS bujn-winpc <cmd>` or just a normal `ssh bhyslop@rocket`):

1. **Create the account** (matches what step 3 would do, minus mkpasswd which Cygwin auto-handles in modern installs):
   ```
   net.exe user bujuw_user "" /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes
   ```
   Or pick any non-admin name; just keep it consistent with what BUJB_workload_user expects if you want `tt/buw-jws` to drive it (`bujuw_user`).

2. **Drop the authorized_keys at the default location**, with the forced-command directive baked in:
   ```
   command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"" ssh-ed25519 AAAA…<workload pubkey> bhyslop@mac
   ```
   File: `C:\Users\bujuw_user\.ssh\authorized_keys`, ACLs so only SYSTEM + bujuw_user can read.

3. **Verify Cygwin** at `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe` (the constant `BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd` assumes this path).

Then `tt/buw-jws bujn-winpc` already does the right thing — it SSHs as `bujuw_user@rocket` with the workload key and asks for `bash -i`, which the forced-command directive runs inside Cygwin's `bash --login`. You land in a Cygwin shell as an unprivileged user. The existing `tt/buw-jwk` and `tt/buw-jwc` tabtargets will also work (knock / command-file).

## What this skips

- Caparison Phase 1: no sshd_config edits, no `PasswordAuthentication no`, no admin authkeys lockdown via `icacls`, no Match block ProgramData relocation
- Caparison Phase 3: no WSL `rbtww-main` staging, no powercfg, no Tailscale auto-start
- Garrison's destructive `obliterate_workload` (which requires that Windows reboot primitive — the load-bearer for ₢A-ABG/ABJ/ABK)
- Invigilate gate (would otherwise refuse to proceed if any posture fact mismatched)

## One trade-off worth flagging

`tt/buw-jws` calls `bujp_preflight` indirectly via `bujb_resolve_investiture` — let me confirm by reading the preflight to see if it'll block on caparison-missing facts. Want me to check, or is "expect to disable/bypass one preflight assertion" already acceptable for a hack?

content.text:
Current state on rocket — the workload SSH path is already wired up, just pointing at WSL instead of Cygwin:

| Probe | Result |
|---|---|
| Cygwin bash | Present at `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe` (854 KB, dated 01/23/2024) |
| `bujuw_user` | Active Windows account, member of `*Users` only (unprivileged), password-not-required, last logon 5/11/2026 |
| sshd_config hardening | Already applied — `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` |
| sshd_config Match blocks | `Match Group administrators` → `administrators_authorized_keys` (standard); `Match User bujuw_user` → `__PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/bujuw_user/authorized_keys` (caparison-installed) |
| Workload authorized_keys | Exists, contains workload pubkey with **WSL** `command=` directive: `C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user bujuw_user --exec /bin/bash -lc "$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND"` |

The host was last garrisoned with shell-letter `w` (WSL), not `c` (Cygwin). The whole machinery is already in place — only the `command=` directive content differs between the two.

## The minimal hack

To flip workload SSH from WSL to Cygwin: **one line replacement** in `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\bujuw_user\authorized_keys`. Same pubkey, swap the directive:

From:
```
command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user bujuw_user --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,...
```

To:
```
command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,...
```

That's `BUJB_command_w` → `BUJB_command_c` from bujb_jurisdiction.sh:167-168, with the same pubkey trailing it.

## What to do

Two natural next steps:

options.label: Flip to Cygwin directly
options.description: Replace `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\bujuw_user\authorized_keys` content now to point at Cygwin. Then test with `tt/buw-jws bujn-winpc`.

options.label: Just probe more first
options.description: More reconnaissance before any mutation — e.g., check whether the workload Cygwin home `/cygdrive/c/Users/bujuw_user/` is provisioned (it would be if garrison-c had ever run; only garrison-w landed here, so probably not yet).

596	zbujb_admin_powershell() {
597	  zbujb_sentinel
598	  test $# -ge 2 \
599	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: CHIT BODY required"
600	  local -r z_chit="$1"; shift
601	  local -r z_body="$*"
602	  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"   # WSp-109
603	
604	  zbujb_chit_open "${z_chit}"
605	  local z_exit=0
606	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
607	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
608	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
609	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
610	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \

file.content:
zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 2 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: CHIT BODY required"
  local -r z_chit="$1"; shift
  local -r z_body="$*"
  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"   # WSp-109

  zbujb_chit_open "${z_chit}"
  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body_escaped}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\"" \
      > "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDERR}" \
    || z_exit=$?
  zbujb_diag_dump_pair "${z_chit}" "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDOUT}" "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDERR}"
  return ${z_exit}
}

# zbujb_admin_powershell_with_password_fallback CHIT BODY -- mirror of
# zbujb_admin_powershell but with BatchMode=no and
# PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password so ssh can fall through
# to a /dev/tty password prompt on first-caparison-ever when the admin
# pubkey is not yet authorized. The caparison process never sees the
# password; the prompt is handled by the operator's local ssh client.
# Bounded to a single call site: caparison-windows phase 1 step 3
# (admin pubkey install via single Add-Content) gated by the key-auth
# CDD probe; every other admin-shell op uses the key-only
# zbujb_admin_powershell. Same discipline prelude/postlude, same
# WSp-105 single-cmdlet body discipline, same WSp-109 escape transform,
# same internal forensic capture as zbujb_admin_powershell.
zbujb_admin_powershell_with_password_fallback() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 2 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell_with_password_fallback: CHIT BODY required"
  local -r z_chit="$1"; shift
  local -r z_body="$*"
  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"   # WSp-109

  zbujb_chit_open "${z_chit}"
  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"             \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                  \
      -o BatchMode=no                              \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"       \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"       \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body_escaped}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\"" \
      > "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDERR}" \
    || z_exit=$?
  zbujb_diag_dump_pair "${z_chit}" "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDOUT}" "${ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDERR}"
  return ${z_exit}
}

# zbujb_powershell_capture ROLE BODY -- run a single PS expression on the
# remote node as the named role and emit its stdout. Windows CR is stripped
# via bash parameter expansion (no subprocess) so $(...) yields a clean
# value. Single-expression discipline per WSG-PS-5; multi-statement bodies
# do not belong here. Caller || buc_die on capture failure.
# ROLE: zbujb_privileged | zbujb_workload
zbujb_powershell_capture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 2 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_powershell_capture: ROLE BODY required"
  local -r z_role="$1"; shift
  local -r z_body="$*"
  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"   # WSp-109

  local z_key z_user
  case "${z_role}" in
    zbujb_privileged) z_key="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"; z_user="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}" ;;
    zbujb_workload)   z_key="${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}";   z_user="${BUJB_workload_user}"   ;;
    *)                buc_die "zbujb_powershell_capture: unknown role '${z_role}' (zbujb_privileged|zbujb_workload)" ;;
  esac

  local z_out z_exit=0
  z_out=$(ssh -i "${z_key}"                       \
              "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"         \
              -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"  \
              -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}" \
              "${z_user}@${BURN_HOST}"            \
              "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body_escaped}\"") \
    || z_exit=$?

  printf '%s' "${z_out//$'\r'/}"
  return "${z_exit}"
}

# zbujb_workload_ssh CHIT REMOTE_CMD -- execute a Windows-native command
# line as the workload user via SSH. The default Windows OpenSSH shell is
# cmd.exe; the command line goes through cmd.exe's argv layer to the named
# binary. Used by garrison-w for `wsl.exe --import` (which writes to the
# workload's HKCU\Lxss because the SSH session is a real workload logon)
# and `wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --user root bash -c "..."`
# invocations that follow the import. stdout/stderr land at indexed paths
# exposed via ZBUJB_LAST_AP_STDOUT / _STDERR (shared CHIT-capture pattern
# with the admin helpers); returns ssh's exit code.
zbujb_workload_ssh() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 2 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_workload_ssh: CHIT REMOTE_CMD required"
  local -r z_chit="$1"; shift
  local -r z_remote_cmd="$*"

  zbujb_chit_open "${z_chit}"

| `rbtww-main` (admin's copy) | Has no Linux user `bujuw_user` (seed-only — matches design intent of ₢A-ABJ admin-no-WSL, except admin still has it registered) |
| Workload SSH (knock) | Exit 0 — chain works |
| Workload SSH (whoami) | Exit 0, **empty stdout** — confirms the `command=` directive's WSL invocation drops output (UTF-16LE mangling, no `WSL_UTF8=1` set in the directive). Output dropping is a known wart, not a fresh break. |

So the existing WSL workload path is wired but transport-lossy. Cygwin would side-step the UTF-16 problem entirely (ASCII/UTF-8 over stdio, no recoding by `wsl.exe`).

## The `brad@rocket` hack plan

Your proposal — fresh unprivileged account `brad`, shell = Cygwin bash — is the cleanest option. It side-steps the formal machinery entirely (doesn't perturb `bujuw_user`, no garrison ceremony, no caparison touches). Let me lay out what it needs and ask one decision before doing anything mutating.

**Steps once you say go**:

1. Create Windows account on rocket:
   ```
   net.exe user brad "" /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes
   ```
   (`Users` group by default — unprivileged.)

2. Add a sshd_config Match block routing `brad` to a known location (or skip Match and use the user-default `C:\Users\brad\.ssh\authorized_keys`).

3. Drop authorized_keys with the Cygwin forced-command directive + your chosen pubkey:
   ```
   command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding ssh-ed25519 AAAA… brad@mac
   ```

4. Ensure ACLs on the authkeys file pass sshd's strict-mode checks (SYSTEM + brad full; nothing else).

**One key question** before I touch anything:

aiTitle: Enable SSH access to Rocket Cygwin shells

content.text:
Quote-escaping the C# inline definition through cmd→PowerShell→Add-Type is wedging on the BCG/WSG triple-transport stack. Two cleaner paths now:

**Option A — base64-encoded PowerShell**: Build a properly-encoded `-EncodedCommand` blob from the Mac side that bypasses cmd.exe and PowerShell quoting entirely. The CreateProfile P/Invoke runs cleanly. One more attempt at this.

**Option B — sshd_config Match block (caparison's pattern)**: Append three lines to sshd_config routing `brad`'s authkeys to an absolute path `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys` (no `%h`, no profile dependency). Restart sshd. This is what your existing code does for `bujuw_user` — proven pattern, fully reversible by removing those three lines.

The profile-creation route avoids touching sshd_config but is fighting the transport quoting. The Match block route is two more remote ops total.

questions.question: How do you want to proceed past the profile-not-registered obstacle?

options.label: Try base64-encoded PS for CreateProfile
options.description: One more attempt at force-creating brad's profile, via -EncodedCommand. Avoids any sshd_config edit. Risk: more transport fiddling; the P/Invoke might also have ACL/quirks of its own.

options.label: Switch to Match block (caparison's pattern)
options.description: Append a Match User brad block to sshd_config pointing to C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys, move the authkeys there, restart sshd. Mirrors what your existing code does for bujuw_user. Three remote ops, all reversible.

options.label: Abandon brad, just patch bujuw_user
options.description: Drop the brad approach. Edit bujuw_user's existing authkeys to swap the WSL command= directive for the Cygwin one. No new account, no sshd_config edit, no profile issue. Uses tt/buw-jws as-is. Downside: perturbs the bujuw_user the formal heat machinery owns.

answers.How do you want to proceed past the prof: Hold on, I haven't been following along.  State clearly what the issue is, and what ramifications there are for the options you recommend considering.

content.text:
## The actual issue (plain)

Windows OpenSSH can't find brad's `authorized_keys` file even though it exists at the right path with right contents and right ACLs. Here's the chain:

- sshd_config has the default `AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys` — a **relative path**
- "Relative to what?" → brad's home directory
- "Where's brad's home?" → sshd asks Windows via `GetUserProfileDirectoryW()`
- Windows answers from registry: `HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList\{brad-SID}\ProfileImagePath`
- Brad has a SID (`S-1-5-21-…-1031`) but **no entry in ProfileList** because he's never interactively logged in
- Windows returns "no home"; sshd has nothing to read; auth fails

We confirmed that registry gap directly. The directory `C:\Users\brad\` exists, but the *registration* doesn't. Creating the directory manually as admin doesn't register the profile.

## What your existing code does

Caparison-windows hits this exact problem for `bujuw_user` and solves it by inserting:

```
Match User bujuw_user
    AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/bujuw_user/authorized_keys
```

An **absolute** path. sshd never asks "what's the home" — it just opens the file. Microsoft documents this as the recommended pattern for headless service accounts (accounts that never log in interactively).

## The three paths and their ramifications

**1 — Force-create brad's profile (preserves "no sshd_config edit")**
- One Windows API call (`userenv.dll!CreateProfile`) to register brad in ProfileList
- After it succeeds: brad is a "real" Windows profile, `%h` resolves normally, current authkeys works as-is
- Friction now: the API call has to traverse cmd.exe → PowerShell → inline C# `Add-Type` — three quoting layers; we hit one already. Reliable form is a base64-encoded `-EncodedCommand` PowerShell blob built on the Mac side. Should be solvable in one more try.
- Permanent footprint: brad's profile lives in ProfileList forever (until you explicitly remove it via `Remove-CimInstance Win32_UserProfile` or similar). Slightly heavier teardown than just `net user /delete`.

**2 — Match block (caparison's proven pattern)**
- Append three lines to sshd_config, move authkeys to `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys`, restart sshd
- Works exactly like bujuw_user does today — no profile dependency
- Permanent footprint until you remove the lines + sshd restart + delete brad
- Caveat: any future re-run of `caparison-windows` could touch sshd_config; whether it's idempotent against an extra Match block depends on how caparison's append is written. Worth checking before doing it, but likely fine since caparison appends specific text rather than rewriting the file.
- Restart sshd does kick admin sessions briefly (you'd reconnect on next call)

**3 — Hijack `bujuw_user` (no new account at all)**
- Replace bujuw_user's existing `authorized_keys` content — swap WSL directive for Cygwin directive
- Uses every piece already in place: the Match block, the ACLs, the user account, even `tt/buw-jws` 
- Ramification: bujuw_user is "the workload user" in the heat's worldview. Hijacking it means whenever you (or the heat) run garrison-w/garrison-c next, your change gets clobbered (garrison rewrites authkeys from scratch). Reversibility = re-run garrison.
- Goes against your original framing of "isolated test account, don't perturb the formal stuff"

## My honest read

You picked "no sshd_config edit" expecting it to be the lower-touch path. For an account that's **never logged in interactively**, it's actually the harder path — Windows OpenSSH's `%h` resolution is gated on profile registration, which is exactly what fresh-via-`net user` accounts don't have.

Option 2 is what your codebase already uses and is the path of least surprise. Option 1 is achievable but more work for the same end-state. Option 3 is shortest but breaks the isolation property you wanted.

Want me to verify caparison's sshd_config append is benign before going with Option 2, or do you want to pick now?

It's on rocket — `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` is the Windows OpenSSH server's config file on the remote machine, not anything in this repo. We've been reading it via admin SSH all along.

**No file in this repo would change for Option 2.** No regime entries for brad, no tabtarget, no zipper enrollment, no BURN/BURP profile. The Match block lives entirely on rocket. From the Mac side you'd just `ssh brad@rocket` directly.

The relevant question is the *other* coupling: rocket's `sshd_config` is a file that `caparison-windows` actively manages. A Match block I add for brad shares the file with caparison's blocks. That's not coupling between repo and rocket — it's coupling between the hack and the formal machinery, both living on rocket.

Whether that matters depends on how caparison writes sshd_config. Let me check before saying more.

file.content:
buc_step "  [Phase 1] [Step 6/13] Build new sshd_config content (curia-side: replace hardening directives + strip prior Match block + append canonical Match block)"
  local z_current_raw=""
  z_current_raw=$(<"${z_sshd_current_stdout}")
  local z_current_clean="${z_current_raw//$'\r'/}"

  local z_directives_roll=()
  local z_pair=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"

  local z_current_lines_roll=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_current_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_current_clean}"

  local z_out_lines_roll=()
  local z_seen_roll=()
  local z_skip_block=0
  local z_i=0 z_j=0 z_k=0
  local z_directive="" z_value="" z_check="" z_first_word=""
  local z_is_directive=0 z_already_seen=0
  for z_i in "${!z_current_lines_roll[@]}"; do
    z_line="${z_current_lines_roll[$z_i]}"

    case "${z_line}" in
      "Match User ${BUJB_workload_user}")
        z_skip_block=1
        continue
        ;;
      "Match "*)
        z_skip_block=0
        ;;
    esac
    test "${z_skip_block}" -eq 1 && continue

    z_check="${z_line#"${z_line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    z_check="${z_check#\#}"
    z_check="${z_check#"${z_check%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    z_first_word="${z_check%% *}"

    z_is_directive=0
    for z_j in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
      z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_j]%% *}"
      z_value="${z_directives_roll[$z_j]#* }"
      if [[ "${z_first_word}" == "${z_directive}" ]]; then
        z_already_seen=0
        for z_k in "${!z_seen_roll[@]}"; do
          test "${z_seen_roll[$z_k]}" = "${z_directive}" && { z_already_seen=1; break; }
        done
        if [[ "${z_already_seen}" -eq 0 ]]; then
          z_out_lines_roll+=("${z_directive} ${z_value}")
          z_seen_roll+=("${z_directive}")
        fi
        z_is_directive=1
        break
      fi
    done

content.text:
Read caparison's sshd_config rewriter at bujb_jurisdiction.sh:1700-1760.

**Caparison only strips its own `Match User bujuw_user` block** (line 1728) — any other Match block passes through untouched (line 1732-1734 explicitly resets the skip flag on a different Match line). The hardening directives (`PubkeyAuthentication`, `PasswordAuthentication`, `PermitEmptyPasswords`) are replaced; everything else is preserved verbatim.

So if I add `Match User brad / AuthorizedKeysFile ...`, the next time caparison runs, **brad's Match block survives intact**. They share the file but don't interfere.

## Coupling, plainly stated

| Coupling axis | Option 1 (CreateProfile) | Option 2 (Match block) | Option 3 (hijack bujuw_user) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Files in this repo | None | None | None |
| Files on rocket touched | Registry `HKLM\…\ProfileList\{SID}`; `C:\Users\brad\` becomes registered profile | One file: `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` (append 3 lines); one new file `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys` | `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\bujuw_user\authorized_keys` (one line replaced) |
| Coupling with formal heat machinery | None | Shares sshd_config file with caparison, but caparison ignores other Match blocks | High — clobbered by next garrison run |
| Future-caparison safety | Brad's profile registration untouched | Brad's Match block preserved verbatim | Workload directive gets rewritten back to WSL on next garrison-w |

The repo concern you raised has zero issue across all three. The remaining question is rocket-side, and Option 2's sshd_config sharing is mild: caparison literally walks the file looking for `Match User bujuw_user` and ignores everything else.

## My recommendation

**Option 2.** The "coupling" is two parties writing different parts of the same file without overlap — caparison owns its directives and its own Match block; brad gets a separate Match block caparison never touches. Removing brad later is: delete the three lines, `sshd -t`, restart sshd, delete the authkeys file, `net user brad /delete`. All reversible, no repo footprint, brad stays fully headless (no Windows profile registered).

The reason against Option 1 isn't transport difficulty — it's that registering brad's profile makes brad less isolated, not more. Profile registration creates a Windows hive, login-screen presence behavior, etc. The Match-block-with-headless-account is exactly Microsoft's documented pattern for service-style accounts that should never have a profile.

Want me to go ahead with Option 2?

Restate without conflating the future 'caparison' vocabulary: do I need to customize sshd_config on my macos computer here to cause the right thing to happen when I ssh brad@rocket?  I don't understand Match blocks.  I'm also leery of setting up a shell environment that has byzantine complexities due to layers of escaping, etc.  I really really want any computer on my network able to `ssh brad@rocket` and get as vanilla a shell as possible with cygwin. content.text:
Direct answers first:

**Q: Customize sshd_config on your Mac?** No. Zero changes to your Mac. The Mac's `sshd_config` controls *incoming* SSH connections to the Mac. When you run `ssh brad@rocket`, your Mac is the *client*; its server config is irrelevant. The relevant file is on rocket, the receiver.

**Q: What's a Match block?** A few lines in rocket's SSH server config file (`C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` on rocket — nothing on your Mac) that say "for this specific user, look at *this* file for trusted keys." We need one for brad because Windows OpenSSH has a quirk: it asks the OS "where's brad's home folder?" — and the OS replies "no idea, brad's never logged in." A Match block sidesteps that by giving sshd an absolute file path to use instead of relying on home-folder lookup. It is the standard Microsoft-documented pattern for accounts that should never have a Windows login profile.

**Q: Vanilla Cygwin shell, minimum escaping?** Yes, achievable. With the Match block carrying the file-location info, the trusted-keys file itself becomes one clean line — no `$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND` wrapping, no nested quoting.

## The shape this collapses to

Two pieces of state on rocket. Nothing on your Mac. Nothing in this repo.

**1. Three lines appended to rocket's sshd_config:**
```
Match User brad
    AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/users/brad/authorized_keys
```
(Two content lines plus a leading blank line. Reversible: delete the lines, restart sshd.)

**2. One file at `C:\ProgramData\ssh\users\brad\authorized_keys` containing one line per trusted computer:**
```
command="C:\cygwin64\bin\bash --login" ssh-ed25519 AAAA…<your-mac-pubkey> bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org
```
That's it. Two tokens before the key. No backslash-quote dance, no shell variable.

## The user experience after that

From any computer whose pubkey is in that file:
```
ssh brad@rocket
```
→ vanilla interactive Cygwin login bash prompt. No tabtargets, no scripts, no Tools/-machinery involved.

To enable a new computer later: append one more line to that authorized_keys file (`command="C:\cygwin64\bin\bash --login" ssh-ed25519 …<other-computer-pubkey>`). One line per computer. No other change anywhere.

## On the "two parties writing the same file" concern

Yes, brad's three lines go in the same `sshd_config` file that the formal heat machinery also writes to. I checked the formal machinery's rewriter — it looks for *its own* Match block by exact name and leaves any other Match block alone. So brad's three lines survive future runs of the formal machinery untouched. The risk is not zero (someone could rewrite the file with different logic later), but it's small, and the failure mode is loud (brad stops working, three lines re-added in 30 seconds).

## Trade you should know about

This shape gives you a clean *interactive* Cygwin shell. If you later want `ssh brad@rocket somecommand` to also work (run `somecommand` non-interactively in Cygwin and exit), the one-line directive needs to grow a `$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND` wrapper — exactly the byzantine thing you don't want. The right answer when you reach that point is: add a second line in the authkeys file for a non-interactive use case, or pick the wrapper then. Don't pay for it now.

Want me to go?
